title: Cannot create a zoho e-mail ccount
creator: rdingraham
content: When attempting to set up an e-mail account for Zoho e-mail I get an authentication error. It says: "Unable to log in. Probably wrong configuration, username of password." I disabled Account Hub, tried again, but this did not solve the problem, and I get the same authentication error. I tried several other things, using manual configuration, copying instructions from the Zoho website, but none of them worked. They all ended with the error message, "Thunderbird failed to find the settings for your email account." The server and port settings are correct. The username and password are correct. What is the problem?

title: překlad
creator: annamusilkova
content: Buďte milí a konkrétní. Naši přispěvatelé jsou uživatelé Mozilly jako vy, kteří věnují svůj čas pomoci. Říkat nám: „Videa na YouTube se vždy sekají“ je mnohem užitečnější, než když „je něco rozbité“. https://gemini.google.com/share/08296391b630Be nice and specific. Our contributors are Mozilla users like you who give their time to help. Telling us, "YouTube videos are always cutting out" is much more helpful than "something is broken."

title: Unable to open an attachment in Thunderbird. "Word cannot open the existing file."
creator: rbennett22
content:
I am working on a new laptop running Windows 11. Thunderbird was successfully installed a week ago and was running fine until I made a change related to OneDrive - attempting to remove OneDrive as a default location for files. later, I attempted to open an MS Word attachment to an email and got this message from Microsoft Word: "Unable to open the existing file: (C:\Users\..(myname)..\AppData..\Normal.dotm)".

I think my OneDrive action - removing a OneDrive folder from my C:/Users/..(name)../ location on my laptop - per Windows support directions, caused this. Maybe a disconnect between Thunderbird and MS Word? I can open other file types that are attachments.

Can you help explain and correct this problem?

Thanks

title: Thunderbird Hilfe 1
creator: aaaaa
content:
Good day, thank you in advance for your help.

I have two questions: 1. Which Thunderbird version is better than ESR? Could you share your opinion?

It's faster and it applies all changes more quickly, that's how it seems right now.

If there are still 32-bit and 64-bit versions available simultaneously, I'll test it sometime. I have no idea what kind of channel that would be.

Problem question:

In Thunderbird 64-bit POP3, the font changes to blue for every second email, including in replies. Can you tell me how to display every email in black? Only the second email in the inbox is displayed in blue, so the text isn't blue when receiving emails. When composing, everything looks black. What do I need to change?

Question 2: Regardless of whether it's 32-bit, 64-bit, or any channel, I see updates to the latest version, and in the program itself, there's an option to view older updates, but everything is white. I only see white and nothing else. Is this intentional? I can't see the update, but it's still current?


``` Thanks for the information

title: Insert HTML blocks images
creator: hgarciaperis
content:
I'm using Thunderbird 149.0.2 (64-bit) for windows.

I offen prepare emails in an HTML file and when I'm ok with the result I insert HTML to a new email. Until older versions Thunderbird blocks remote images when I insert HTML, but appears a message at the end of the email warning me that it blocked images and an Option button and I could unblock image by image all the images. But with this version the message still appears but the button doesn't appear and I can't unblock the images.

I think this is a bug and I need to get back that button, because I send messages this way a lot.

Thank you
